product.name: ArWoWear T20 Static Pro Pro
product.titleDescriptor: 79% Cotton 20% Polyester 1% Carbon Twill, Antisatic Fabric, 260 gr/m'2
product.specLine: 260 g/m², 79% Cotton 20% Polyester 1% Carbon, Antistatic, 3/1S Twill Fabric
descriptions: ArWoWear T20 Flame Arc Pro is light weight 260 g/m², 79% Cotton 20% Polyester 1% Carbon, vat dyed, antistatic workwear fabric. Due to carbon fibers used on the back side of the fabric it has antistatic certification. (EN 1149-5)

It is suitable to use on electronic industry where static electric formation is a problem. Oil and gas industry platform workers can also use it to prevent static electric related explosions on dangerous area.
79% Cotton 20% Polyester 1% Carbon Twill, Antisatic Fabric, 260 gr/m'2
260 g/m², 79% Cotton 20% Polyester 1% Carbon, Antistatic, 3/1S Twill Fabric
technicalParameters: Fiber | 79% Cotton (CO) 20% Polyester 1% Carbon(CF) | EN ISO 2076
Tensile Strength | Warp>1000N; Weft>800N | EN ISO 13934-1
Tear Strength | Warp>20N; Weft>15N | EN ISO 13937-2
Dimensional Change | -3% < Length < +3% ; -3% < Width < +3% | EN ISO 5077
Dry Cleaning Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 D01
Hot Press Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 X11
Perspiration Light Fast. | 2-3 | EN ISO 105 B07
Azo Test | No Azo Colorants. | EN ISO 14362-1

evidence: Oil and gas industry platform workers can also use it to prevent static electric related explosions on dangerous area.
acceptedMatches.rationale: Product description explicitly states use by oil and gas industry platform workers to prevent static-electricity related explosions, directly connecting the fabric to the energy/electrical-hazard sector.

possibleButNotAccepted.reason: Antistatic FR-adjacent workwear could relate to industrial metalworking environments, but no explicit welding/foundry/metallurgy use is stated.

evidence: 260 g/m², 79% Cotton 20% Polyester 1% Carbon, Antistatic, 3/1S Twill Fabric
primary.rationale: The product is explicitly described as a general antistatic coverall/workwear fabric (categorySourceGroup: Antistatic Twill Workwear Fabrics, Application: Coveralls) without a single narrower sector dominating the product's primary commercial framing, so general industrial workwear is the most defensible primary application.

evidence: Oil and gas industry platform workers can also use it to prevent static electric related explosions on dangerous area.
secondary.rationale: The description explicitly names oil and gas platform workers as a use case to prevent static-electricity related explosions, independently supporting the energy sector as a secondary application beyond mere antistatic property.
